---
title: "Exploratory Data Analysis Report"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: readable
    mathjax: default
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
params:
  cleaning_stats_agg: NA
  cleaning_stats_subj: NA
  stim_pref_agg: NA
  stim_pref_subj: NA
  stim_pref_test: NA
  reward_rate_by_choice: NA
  reward_rate_over_bins: NA
  feedback_condition_overall: NA
  feedback_condition_by_subject: NA
  rt_summary_agg: NA
  rt_summary_subj: NA
  rt_over_bins: NA
  rt_by_condition_summary: NA
  rt_by_condition_data: NA
  accuracy_agg: NA
  accuracy_subj: NA
  accuracy_test: NA
  accuracy_over_bins: NA
  wsls_by_outcome_subj: NA
  prp_by_outcome_subj: NA
  wsls_test: NA
  wsls_loss_vs_wins_test: NA
  prp_test: NA
  reward_rate_subj: NA
  win_stay_overall_subj: NA
  questionnaire_scores_subj: NA
  questionnaire_scores_long: NA
  questionnaire_score_summaries: NA
  bis_ss_corr: NA
  bis_ss_lm: NA
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(htmltools)

# --- Shared styling helpers (keep changes minimal & consistent) ---
eda_theme_plot2 <- function(base_size = 18) {
  #####
  # Shared ggplot theme matching the “Plot 2” house style
  #
  # Parameters
  # ----
  # base_size : numeric
  #     Base font size (default: 18)
  #
  # Returns
  # ----
  # ggplot2 theme
  #     Theme object to be added to a ggplot.
  #####
  ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = base_size) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      panel.grid.major = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold", size = 20),
      axis.title.x = ggplot2::element_text(size = 18),
      axis.title.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 18),
      axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(size = 16),
      axis.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 16)
    )
}

eda_bar_border <- list(color = "black", linewidth = 0.5)
```

# 1. Data Cleaning Statistics

This section summarizes data quality, including missed trials and trials with unrealistically fast reaction times (e.g., < 200ms).

## 1.1 Aggregate Statistics

```{r cleaning-agg}
if (!is.null(params$cleaning_stats_agg)) {
  params$cleaning_stats_agg %>%
    kable(digits = 2, caption = "Table 1.1: Aggregate Data Cleaning Statistics") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
} else {
  print("No aggregate statistics provided.")
}
```

## 1.2 Subject-Level Statistics

```{r cleaning-subj}
if (!is.null(params$cleaning_stats_subj)) {
  params$cleaning_stats_subj %>%
    dplyr::rename(
      "Subject ID" = participant_id,
      "Total Trials" = total_main_trials,
      "Missed" = n_missed_choice,
      "Fast RT" = n_fast_rt,
      "Valid Trials" = n_valid_trials,
      "Invalid Trials" = n_invalid_trials,
      "Valid (%)" = percent_valid_trials
    ) %>%
    kable(digits = 2, caption = "Table 1.2: Subject-Level Data Cleaning Summary") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    scroll_box(height = "500px")
} else {
  print("No subject-level statistics provided.")
}
```

# 2. Stimulus Preference

This section summarizes whether participants preferred one stimulus (0 vs 1) overall. Missed trials are excluded.

## 2.1 Aggregate

```{r stim-pref-agg-table}
if (!is.null(params$stim_pref_agg)) {
  params$stim_pref_agg %>%
    kable(
      digits = 3,
      caption = "Table 2.1: Aggregate Stimulus Preference (Overall Choice Proportions)"
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
} else {
  print("No aggregate stimulus preference provided.")
}
```

```{r stim-pref-agg-plot}
if (!is.null(params$stim_pref_agg)) {
  agg <- params$stim_pref_agg
  plot_df <- tibble::tibble(
    stimulus = c("Stimulus 0", "Stimulus 1"),
    proportion = c(agg$`Prop Choice Stimulus 0`, agg$`Prop Choice Stimulus 1`)
  )

  p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = stimulus, y = proportion, fill = stimulus)) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.85, width = 0.7, color = eda_bar_border$color, linewidth = eda_bar_border$linewidth) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Stimulus 0" = "dodgerblue", "Stimulus 1" = "firebrick")) +
    labs(
      title = "Overall Proportion of Choices",
      x = "Choice",
      y = "Proportion"
    ) +
    eda_theme_plot2() +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

  print(p)
}
```

## 2.2 Subject-Level

```{r stim-pref-subj-table}
if (!is.null(params$stim_pref_subj)) {
  params$stim_pref_subj %>%
    dplyr::rename(
      "Subject ID" = participant_id,
      "Valid Trials" = n_valid_trials,
      "N Choice 0" = n_choice_0,
      "N Choice 1" = n_choice_1,
      "Prop Choice 0" = prop_choice_0,
      "Prop Choice 1" = prop_choice_1
    ) %>%
    kable(digits = 3, caption = "Table 2.2: Subject-Level Stimulus Preference") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    scroll_box(height = "500px")
} else {
  print("No subject-level stimulus preference provided.")
}
```

```{r stim-pref-subj-plot, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(params$stim_pref_subj)) {
  subj <- params$stim_pref_subj

  .make_pref_plot <- function(df, prop_col, stim_label, fill_col) {
    #####
    # Create a subject-level preference histogram plot (counts)
    #
    # Parameters
    # ----
    # df : tibble
    #     Subject-level preference data frame
    # prop_col : character
    #     Column name containing preference proportion (e.g., "prop_choice_0")
    # stim_label : character
    #     Label used in the plot title (e.g., "Stimulus 0")
    # fill_col : character
    #     Fill color used for the histogram
    #
    # Returns
    # ----
    # ggplot
    #     Histogram plot with participant counts on y-axis
    #####
    x <- df[[prop_col]]
    m <- mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)

    ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[prop_col]])) +
      geom_histogram(
        aes(y = after_stat(count)),
        binwidth = 0.05,
        fill = fill_col,
        color = "black",
        alpha = 0.85,
        boundary = 0
      ) +
      geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.9) +
      geom_vline(xintercept = m, color = "grey10", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1.1) +
      annotate(
        "text",
        x = min(0.74, m + 0.01),
        y = Inf,
        label = paste0("Mean: ", scales::percent(m, accuracy = 0.1)),
        hjust = 0,
        vjust = 1.6,
        color = "grey10",
        size = 6
      ) +
      scale_x_continuous(
        labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
        limits = c(0.25, 0.75),
        breaks = seq(0.25, 0.75, by = 0.10)
      ) +
      labs(
        title = paste0("Subject-Level Preference for ", stim_label),
        x = if (prop_col == "prop_choice_0") expression(italic(p)(choice == 0)) else expression(italic(p)(choice == 1)),
        y = expression(italic(N))
      ) +
      eda_theme_plot2()
  }

  p0 <- .make_pref_plot(subj, "prop_choice_0", "Stimulus 0", "dodgerblue")
  p1 <- .make_pref_plot(subj, "prop_choice_1", "Stimulus 1", "firebrick")

  # Render as a simple carousel (robust in HTML via embedded image URIs)
  tmp0 <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  tmp1 <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  ggsave(tmp0, plot = p0, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 160)
  ggsave(tmp1, plot = p1, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 160)

  img0 <- knitr::image_uri(tmp0)
  img1 <- knitr::image_uri(tmp1)

  carousel_id <- paste0("stim-pref-carousel-", as.integer(stats::runif(1, 1, 1e9)))

  htmltools::browsable(
    htmltools::tagList(
      htmltools::tags$style(htmltools::HTML("
        .eda-carousel-wrap { max-width: 920px; margin: 8px 0 18px 0; }
        .eda-carousel {
          display: flex;
          align-items: center;
          gap: 10px;
        }
        .eda-carousel .viewport { flex: 1 1 auto; }
        .eda-carousel .slide { display: none; }
        .eda-carousel .slide.active { display: block; }
        .eda-carousel img { width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px; }
        .eda-carousel button.nav {
          width: 44px; height: 44px; border-radius: 22px;
          border: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.25);
          background: rgba(255,255,255,0.85);
          color: rgba(0,0,0,0.75);
          font-size: 26px; line-height: 40px;
          cursor: pointer;
          box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.12);
          user-select: none;
          flex: 0 0 auto;
        }
        .eda-carousel button.nav:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.95); }
        .eda-carousel .caption { text-align: center; margin-top: 6px; color: rgba(0,0,0,0.65); font-size: 14px; }
      ")),
      htmltools::tags$div(
        class = "eda-carousel-wrap",
        htmltools::tags$div(
          id = carousel_id,
          class = "eda-carousel",
          `data-idx` = "0",
          htmltools::tags$button(
            class = "nav prev",
            type = "button",
            onclick = sprintf("edaCarouselStep('%s', -1)", carousel_id),
            htmltools::HTML("&#10094;")
          ),
          htmltools::tags$div(
            class = "viewport",
            htmltools::tags$div(class = "slide active", htmltools::tags$img(src = img0, alt = "Stimulus 0 preference plot")),
            htmltools::tags$div(class = "slide", htmltools::tags$img(src = img1, alt = "Stimulus 1 preference plot"))
          ),
          htmltools::tags$button(
            class = "nav next",
            type = "button",
            onclick = sprintf("edaCarouselStep('%s', 1)", carousel_id),
            htmltools::HTML("&#10095;")
          )
        ),
        htmltools::tags$div(class = "caption", "Use arrows to switch: Stimulus 0 ↔ Stimulus 1")
      ),
      htmltools::tags$script(htmltools::HTML("
        function edaCarouselShow(id, idx) {
          const el = document.getElementById(id);
          if (!el) return;
          const slides = el.querySelectorAll('.slide');
          const n = slides.length;
          const i = ((idx % n) + n) % n;
          slides.forEach((s, k) => s.classList.toggle('active', k === i));
          el.setAttribute('data-idx', String(i));
        }
        function edaCarouselStep(id, delta) {
          const el = document.getElementById(id);
          if (!el) return;
          const cur = parseInt(el.getAttribute('data-idx') || '0', 10);
          edaCarouselShow(id, cur + delta);
        }
      "))
    )
  )
}
```

## 2.3 Significance Test (Difference in Proportions)

We test whether participants’ stimulus 1 choice proportion differs from chance (0.5) using a one-sample t-test on subject-level proportions.

```{r stim-pref-test}
if (!is.null(params$stim_pref_test)) {
  params$stim_pref_test %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      `\\(\\mathbf{N}\\)` = n,
      `\\(\\mathbf{\\mu_0}\\)` = mu,
      `\\(\\mathbf{\\bar{x}}\\)` = mean_prop_choice_1,
      `\\(\\mathbf{SD}\\)` = sd_prop_choice_1,
      `\\(\\mathbf{\\Delta}\\)` = mean_diff_from_mu,
      `\\(\\mathbf{t}\\)` = t,
      `\\(\\mathbf{df}\\)` = df,
      `\\(\\mathbf{p}\\)` = p_value,
      `\\(\\mathbf{2.5\\%}\\)` = conf_low,
      `\\(\\mathbf{97.5\\%}\\)` = conf_high
    ) %>%
    kable(
      digits = 4,
      escape = FALSE,
      caption = "Table 2.3: One-sample t-test of Subject-Level Preference for Stimulus 1 vs Chance (0.5)"
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    row_spec(0, bold = FALSE)
} else {
  print("No stimulus preference test results provided.")
}
```

# 3. Reward, Feedback, and Learning

This section provides descriptive checks on outcomes and feedback exposure: reward rate by chosen stimulus, reward rate over time, and the distribution of feedback conditions.

## 3.1 Aggregate

```{r reward-by-choice-table}
if (!is.null(params$reward_rate_by_choice)) {
  params$reward_rate_by_choice %>%
    dplyr::select(choice_label, n_trials_for_choice, n_rewards, reward_rate) %>%
    dplyr::rename(
      "Choice" = choice_label,
      "N Trials" = n_trials_for_choice,
      "N Rewards" = n_rewards,
      "Reward Rate" = reward_rate
    ) %>%
    kable(digits = 3, caption = "Table 3.1: Reward Rate by Chosen Stimulus (Valid Trials)") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
} else {
  print("No reward-rate-by-choice summary provided.")
}
```

```{r reward-feedback-agg-plots, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(params$reward_rate_by_choice) &&
    !is.null(params$reward_rate_over_bins) &&
    !is.null(params$feedback_condition_overall)) {

  # ---- Plot 1: Reward rate by chosen stimulus ----
  rr <- params$reward_rate_by_choice %>%
    dplyr::mutate(choice_label = factor(choice_label, levels = c("Stimulus 0", "Stimulus 1")))

  p_rr_choice <- ggplot(rr, aes(x = choice_label, y = reward_rate, fill = choice_label)) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.85, width = 0.7, color = eda_bar_border$color, linewidth = eda_bar_border$linewidth) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Stimulus 0" = "dodgerblue", "Stimulus 1" = "firebrick")) +
    labs(
      title = "Overall Reward Rate by Chosen Stimulus",
      x = "Stimulus Choice",
      y = "Reward Rate"
    ) +
    eda_theme_plot2() +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

  tmp_rr_choice <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  ggsave(tmp_rr_choice, plot = p_rr_choice, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 170)

  # ---- Plot 2: Mean reward rate over trial bins ----
  rb <- params$reward_rate_over_bins
  p_rr_bins <- ggplot(rb, aes(x = trial_bin)) +
    {if ("mean_optimal_reward_prob_bin" %in% names(rb) && any(is.finite(rb$mean_optimal_reward_prob_bin)))
      geom_line(aes(y = mean_optimal_reward_prob_bin, color = "Optimal reward prob (max)"), alpha = 0.65, linewidth = 0.9)
    } +
    geom_line(aes(y = mean_reward_rate_bin, color = "Observed reward rate"), linewidth = 1) +
    geom_point(aes(y = mean_reward_rate_bin, color = "Observed reward rate"), size = 2) +
    geom_errorbar(
      aes(ymin = mean_reward_rate_bin - sem_reward_rate_bin, ymax = mean_reward_rate_bin + sem_reward_rate_bin),
      width = 0.2,
      color = "darkgreen",
      alpha = 0.5
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1)) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c(
        "Observed reward rate" = "darkgreen",
        "Optimal reward prob (max)" = "grey40"
      )
    ) +
    labs(
      title = "Mean Reward Rate Over Trial Bins",
      x = "Trial Bin (10 bins)",
      y = "Mean Reward Rate ± SEM",
      color = NULL
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(rb$trial_bin))) +
    eda_theme_plot2(base_size = 14) +
    theme(legend.position = "top")

  tmp_rr_bins <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  ggsave(tmp_rr_bins, plot = p_rr_bins, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 170)

  # ---- Plot 3: Proportion of feedback conditions (valid trials) ----
  fc <- params$feedback_condition_overall %>%
    dplyr::filter(condition_label %in% c("Loss", "Non-Salient Win", "Salient")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      condition_label = factor(condition_label, levels = c("Loss", "Non-Salient Win", "Salient"))
    )

  p_fc <- ggplot(fc, aes(x = condition_label, y = prop, fill = condition_label)) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.85, width = 0.75, color = eda_bar_border$color, linewidth = eda_bar_border$linewidth) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(
      "Loss" = "grey70",
      "Non-Salient Win" = "white",
      "Salient" = "gold"
    )) +
    labs(
      title = "Proportion of Feedback Conditions (Valid Trials)",
      x = NULL,
      y = "Proportion"
    ) +
    eda_theme_plot2() +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

  tmp_fc <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  ggsave(tmp_fc, plot = p_fc, width = 7, height = 4.5, dpi = 170)

  img1 <- knitr::image_uri(tmp_rr_choice)
  img2 <- knitr::image_uri(tmp_rr_bins)
  img3 <- knitr::image_uri(tmp_fc)

  htmltools::browsable(
    htmltools::tagList(
      htmltools::tags$div(style = "max-width: 820px; margin: 8px 0 18px 0;",
                          htmltools::tags$img(src = img1, style = "width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px;")),
      htmltools::tags$div(style = "max-width: 920px; margin: 8px 0 18px 0;",
                          htmltools::tags$img(src = img2, style = "width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px;")),
      htmltools::tags$div(style = "max-width: 820px; margin: 8px 0 18px 0;",
                          htmltools::tags$img(src = img3, style = "width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px;"))
    )
  )
}
```

```{r feedback-agg-table}
if (!is.null(params$feedback_condition_overall)) {
  params$feedback_condition_overall %>%
    dplyr::filter(condition_label %in% c("Loss", "Non-Salient Win", "Salient")) %>%
    dplyr::rename(
      "Condition" = condition_label,
      "N Trials" = n,
      "Proportion" = prop
    ) %>%
    kable(digits = 3, caption = "Table 3.2: Feedback Condition Proportions (Valid Trials)") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
} else {
  print("No feedback-condition proportions provided.")
}
```

## 3.2 Subject-Level

```{r feedback-by-subject-plot, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(params$feedback_condition_by_subject)) {
  df <- params$feedback_condition_by_subject %>%
    dplyr::filter(condition_label %in% c("Loss", "Non-Salient Win", "Salient")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      condition_label = factor(condition_label, levels = c("Loss", "Non-Salient Win", "Salient"))
    )

  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = participant_id, y = prop, fill = condition_label)) +
    geom_col(position = "stack", color = eda_bar_border$color, linewidth = eda_bar_border$linewidth) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(
      "Loss" = "grey70",
      "Non-Salient Win" = "white",
      "Salient" = "gold"
    )) +
    labs(
      title = "Proportion of Feedback Conditions by Subject (Valid Trials)",
      x = "Subject ID",
      y = "Proportion of Trials",
      fill = "Condition"
    ) +
    eda_theme_plot2(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )

  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  ggsave(tmp, plot = p, width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 170)
  img <- knitr::image_uri(tmp)

  htmltools::browsable(
    htmltools::tags$div(
      style = "max-width: 1100px; margin: 8px 0 18px 0;",
      htmltools::tags$img(src = img, style = "width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px;")
    )
  )
}
```

```{r feedback-by-subject-table}
if (!is.null(params$feedback_condition_by_subject)) {
  df <- params$feedback_condition_by_subject %>%
    dplyr::filter(condition_label %in% c("Loss", "Non-Salient Win", "Salient")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      participant_id = as.character(participant_id),
      condition_key = dplyr::case_when(
        condition_label == "Loss" ~ "loss",
        condition_label == "Non-Salient Win" ~ "non_salient_win",
        condition_label == "Salient" ~ "salient",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(condition_key)) %>%
    dplyr::select(participant_id, condition_key, n, prop)

  wide <- df %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      names_from = condition_key,
      values_from = c(n, prop),
      values_fill = list(n = 0, prop = 0)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(as.integer(participant_id)) %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      "Subject ID" = participant_id,
      "N Loss" = n_loss,
      "N Non-salient win" = n_non_salient_win,
      "N Salient" = n_salient,
      "P Loss" = prop_loss,
      "P Non-salient win" = prop_non_salient_win,
      "P Salient" = prop_salient
    )

  wide %>%
    kable(digits = 3, caption = "Table 3.3: Feedback Condition Proportions by Subject (Valid Trials)") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    scroll_box(height = "500px")
} else {
  print("No subject-level feedback-condition proportions provided.")
}
```

# 4. Reaction Time Analysis

This section summarizes reaction times (RTs) on valid-choice trials. We visualize the overall distribution, RT changes over time, and differences by feedback condition. We also inspect subject-level RT distributions and flag participants with unusually frequent very fast responses.

## 4.1 Aggregate

```{r rt-agg-table}
if (!is.null(params$rt_summary_agg)) {
  params$rt_summary_agg %>%
    kable(digits = 3, caption = "Table 4.1: Aggregate Reaction Time Summary") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
} else {
  print("No aggregate RT summary provided.")
}
```

```{r rt-agg-plots, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(params$rt_by_condition_data) && !is.null(params$rt_over_bins)) {
  # Use the same fast-RT threshold as cleaning statistics (run_analysis.R)
  fast_rt_threshold <- 0.2

  # ---- Plot 1: Overall RT distribution ----
  p_dist <- ggplot(params$rt_by_condition_data, aes(x = reaction_time)) +
    geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), binwidth = 0.05, fill = "cornflowerblue", color = "black", alpha = 0.7, boundary = 0) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.2, fill = "lightblue") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = fast_rt_threshold, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
    annotate(
      "text",
      x = fast_rt_threshold + 0.05,
      y = Inf,
      label = paste0("Fast RT threshold (", fast_rt_threshold * 1000, "ms)"),
      hjust = 0,
      vjust = 1.5,
      color = "red",
      size = 4
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, NA), expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.05))) +
    labs(
      title = "Overall Distribution of Reaction Times (Valid Trials)",
      subtitle = paste0("N = ", nrow(params$rt_by_condition_data), " trials. Red line at ", fast_rt_threshold * 1000, "ms."),
      x = "Reaction Time (seconds)",
      y = "Density"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey30"))

  tmp_dist <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  ggsave(tmp_dist, plot = p_dist, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 170)

  # ---- Plot 2: Median RT over trial bins ----
  bins <- params$rt_over_bins
  p_bins <- ggplot(bins, aes(x = trial_bin, y = median_rt)) +
    geom_line(color = "blue", linewidth = 1) +
    geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = median_rt - sem_rt, ymax = median_rt + sem_rt), width = 0.2, color = "blue", alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(
      title = "Median Reaction Time Over Trial Bins",
      x = "Trial Bin (10 bins)",
      y = "Median Reaction Time (s) ± SEM"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(bins$trial_bin))) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

  tmp_bins <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  ggsave(tmp_bins, plot = p_bins, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 170)

  # ---- Plot 3: RT by feedback condition ----
  cond_df <- params$rt_by_condition_data %>%
    dplyr::filter(condition_label %in% c("Salient", "Non-Salient"))

  if (nrow(cond_df) > 5) {
    y_lo <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(cond_df$reaction_time, 0.01, na.rm = TRUE))
    y_hi <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(cond_df$reaction_time, 0.99, na.rm = TRUE))
  } else {
    y_lo <- min(cond_df$reaction_time, na.rm = TRUE)
    y_hi <- max(cond_df$reaction_time, na.rm = TRUE)
  }

  p_cond <- ggplot(cond_df, aes(x = condition_label, y = reaction_time, fill = condition_label)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.06, size = 0.6) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(y_lo, y_hi)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Non-Salient" = "grey70", "Salient" = "gold")) +
    labs(
      title = "Reaction Time by Feedback Condition",
      subtitle = "Valid-choice trials only. Boxes show median & IQR; y-axis trimmed to 1st–99th percentile.",
      x = "Feedback Condition",
      y = "Reaction Time (s)"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(legend.position = "none", plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

  tmp_cond <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  ggsave(tmp_cond, plot = p_cond, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 170)

  # ---- Embed all 3 plots as robust HTML images ----
  img_dist <- knitr::image_uri(tmp_dist)
  img_bins <- knitr::image_uri(tmp_bins)
  img_cond <- knitr::image_uri(tmp_cond)

  htmltools::browsable(
    htmltools::tagList(
      htmltools::tags$div(
        style = "max-width: 920px; margin: 8px 0 18px 0;",
        htmltools::tags$img(src = img_dist, style = "width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px;")
      ),
      htmltools::tags$div(
        style = "max-width: 920px; margin: 8px 0 18px 0;",
        htmltools::tags$img(src = img_bins, style = "width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px;")
      ),
      htmltools::tags$div(
        style = "max-width: 820px; margin: 8px 0 18px 0;",
        htmltools::tags$img(src = img_cond, style = "width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px;")
      )
    )
  )
}
```

```{r rt-by-condition-table}
if (!is.null(params$rt_by_condition_summary)) {
  params$rt_by_condition_summary %>%
    dplyr::rename(
      "Condition" = condition_label,
      "N Trials" = n,
      "Mean RT (s)" = mean_rt,
      "Median RT (s)" = median_rt,
      "SD RT (s)" = sd_rt,
      "Min RT (s)" = min_rt,
      "Max RT (s)" = max_rt
    ) %>%
    kable(digits = 3, caption = "Table 4.2: Reaction Time Summary by Feedback Condition") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
} else {
  print("No RT-by-condition summary provided.")
}
```

## 4.2 Subject-Level

```{r rt-subj-table}
if (!is.null(params$rt_summary_subj)) {
  params$rt_summary_subj %>%
    dplyr::arrange(as.integer(participant_id)) %>%
    dplyr::rename(
      "Subject ID" = participant_id,
      "N Trials (RT available)" = n_trials_rt,
      "Mean RT (s)" = mean_rt,
      "Median RT (s)" = median_rt,
      "SD RT (s)" = sd_rt,
      "Min RT (s)" = min_rt,
      "Max RT (s)" = max_rt,
      "N Fast RT" = n_fast_rt,
      "Fast RT (%)" = percent_fast_rt
    ) %>%
    kable(digits = 3, caption = "Table 4.3: Subject-Level Reaction Time Summary") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    scroll_box(height = "500px")
} else {
  print("No subject-level RT summary provided.")
}
```

```{r rt-subj-hist, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(params$rt_by_condition_data) && !is.null(params$rt_summary_subj)) {
  fast_rt_threshold <- 0.2

  rt_trials <- params$rt_by_condition_data %>%
    dplyr::select(participant_id, reaction_time) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(reaction_time))

  # Highlight participants with >5% very fast RTs (legacy heuristic)
  rt_annot <- params$rt_summary_subj %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      label_text = paste0(round(percent_fast_rt, 1), "%"),
      highlight_flag = percent_fast_rt > 5
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(participant_id, label_text, highlight_flag)

  rt_plot_df <- rt_trials %>%
    dplyr::left_join(rt_annot, by = "participant_id")

  n_subjects <- dplyr::n_distinct(rt_plot_df$participant_id)
  ncol <- 4
  nrow <- max(1, ceiling(n_subjects / ncol))
  plot_height <- max(7, 3 * nrow)

  p_subj <- ggplot(rt_plot_df, aes(x = reaction_time)) +
    geom_histogram(
      aes(y = after_stat(density), fill = highlight_flag),
      binwidth = 0.1,
      boundary = 0,
      color = "black",
      alpha = 0.7
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "firebrick", "FALSE" = "grey80"), guide = "none") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = fast_rt_threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "darkred", linewidth = 0.6) +
    geom_text(
      data = rt_annot,
      aes(x = Inf, y = Inf, label = label_text),
      hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.5, size = 5, alpha = 0.7, fontface = "bold", color = "black",
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    facet_wrap(~ participant_id, scales = "free_y", ncol = ncol) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      limits = c(0, max(rt_plot_df$reaction_time, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.05),
      expand = c(0.01, 0.01)
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.15))) +
    labs(
      title = "Individual Reaction Time Distributions",
      subtitle = "Dashed line: 200ms threshold. Highlighted: >5% trials < 200ms.",
      x = "Reaction Time (s)",
      y = "Density"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1.2)),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey30"),
      strip.background = element_blank(),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
    )

  tmp_subj <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  ggsave(tmp_subj, plot = p_subj, width = 12, height = plot_height, units = "in", dpi = 170)
  img_subj <- knitr::image_uri(tmp_subj)

  htmltools::browsable(
    htmltools::tags$div(
      style = "max-width: 1080px; margin: 10px 0 18px 0;",
      htmltools::tags$img(src = img_subj, style = "width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px;")
    )
  )
}
```

# 5. Accuracy

Accuracy is defined as choosing the option with the higher reward probability on a given trial (ties contribute 0.5). Missed trials and trials with missing reward probabilities are excluded.

## 5.1 Aggregate

```{r accuracy-agg-table}
if (!is.null(params$accuracy_agg)) {
  iqr_val <- if (!is.null(params$accuracy_subj) && "mean_accuracy" %in% names(params$accuracy_subj)) {
    stats::IQR(params$accuracy_subj$mean_accuracy, na.rm = TRUE)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }

  params$accuracy_agg %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      "N" = `N Participants`,
      "Mean" = `Mean Accuracy`,
      "Median" = `Median Accuracy`,
      "SD" = `SD Accuracy`,
      "IQR" = iqr_val,
      "Min" = `Min Accuracy`,
      "Max" = `Max Accuracy`
    ) %>%
    kable(digits = 3, caption = "Table 5.1: Aggregate Accuracy Summary") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
} else {
  print("No aggregate accuracy summary provided.")
}
```

```{r accuracy-ttest}
if (!is.null(params$accuracy_test)) {
  params$accuracy_test %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      `\\(\\mathbf{N}\\)` = n,
      `\\(\\mathbf{\\mu_0}\\)` = mu,
      `\\(\\mathbf{\\bar{x}}\\)` = mean_accuracy,
      `\\(\\mathbf{SD}\\)` = sd_accuracy,
      `\\(\\mathbf{\\Delta}\\)` = mean_diff_from_mu,
      `\\(\\mathbf{t}\\)` = t,
      `\\(\\mathbf{df}\\)` = df,
      `\\(\\mathbf{p}\\)` = p_value,
      `\\(\\mathbf{2.5\\%}\\)` = conf_low,
      `\\(\\mathbf{97.5\\%}\\)` = conf_high
    ) %>%
    kable(
      digits = 4,
      escape = FALSE,
      caption = "Table 5.2: One-sample t-test of Subject-Level Accuracy vs Chance (0.5)"
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    row_spec(0, bold = FALSE)
} else {
  print("No accuracy t-test results provided.")
}
```

```{r accuracy-agg-plots, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(params$accuracy_subj) && !is.null(params$accuracy_test) && !is.null(params$accuracy_over_bins)) {
  subj <- params$accuracy_subj
  tt <- params$accuracy_test
  bins <- params$accuracy_over_bins

  p_value_formatted <- if (is.finite(tt$p_value)) {
    scales::pvalue(tt$p_value, accuracy = .001, add_p = TRUE)
  } else {
    NA_character_
  }

  t_test_caption <- if (!is.finite(tt$p_value)) {
    "Not enough data for t-test (N < 2)"
  } else {
    paste0(
      "One-sample t-test vs 0.5: t(",
      round(tt$df, 1),
      ") = ",
      round(tt$t, 2),
      ", ",
      p_value_formatted
    )
  }

  overall_mean_accuracy <- mean(subj$mean_accuracy, na.rm = TRUE)

  # Boxplot of overall accuracy with (optional) significance bracket
  significance_y_start <- 0.5
  significance_y_end <- overall_mean_accuracy
  significance_x <- 1.3
  annotation_y <- (significance_y_start + significance_y_end) / 2

  p_box <- ggplot(subj, aes(x = factor(1), y = mean_accuracy)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0, size = 1.5, alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_boxplot(width = 0.35, outlier.shape = NA, fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.6) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 18, size = 3, color = "firebrick") +
    {if (is.finite(tt$p_value) && tt$p_value < 0.05)
      list(
        geom_segment(aes(x = significance_x, xend = significance_x, y = significance_y_start, yend = significance_y_end),
                     color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
        geom_segment(aes(x = significance_x - 0.02, xend = significance_x, y = significance_y_end, yend = significance_y_end),
                     color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
        geom_segment(aes(x = significance_x - 0.02, xend = significance_x, y = significance_y_start, yend = significance_y_start),
                     color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
        annotate("text", x = significance_x + 0.05, y = annotation_y,
                 label = p_value_formatted, hjust = 0, size = 4)
      )
    } +
    labs(
      title = "Overall Accuracy Distribution",
      y = "Accuracy",
      x = NULL,
      caption = t_test_caption
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      limits = c(min(0.3, min(subj$mean_accuracy, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.9),
                 max(0.75, max(subj$mean_accuracy, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.05)),
      breaks = seq(0.3, 0.8, by = 0.1)
    ) +
    scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(add = c(0.6, 0.6))) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.x = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x = element_blank()
    )

  tmp_box <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  ggsave(tmp_box, plot = p_box, width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 170)

  # Learning curve
  p_curve <- ggplot(bins, aes(x = trial_bin, y = mean_accuracy_across_subjects)) +
    geom_ribbon(
      aes(ymin = mean_accuracy_across_subjects - sem_accuracy, ymax = mean_accuracy_across_subjects + sem_accuracy),
      fill = "dodgerblue",
      alpha = 0.2
    ) +
    geom_line(color = "dodgerblue", linewidth = 1) +
    geom_point(color = "dodgerblue", size = 2.5) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey30") +
    labs(
      title = "Learning Curve: Accuracy Over Trial Bins",
      x = "Trial Bin",
      y = "Mean Accuracy",
      caption = "Shaded area represents SEM."
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(bins$trial_bin))) +
    eda_theme_plot2(base_size = 14) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))

  tmp_curve <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  ggsave(tmp_curve, plot = p_curve, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 170)

  img_box <- knitr::image_uri(tmp_box)
  img_curve <- knitr::image_uri(tmp_curve)

  htmltools::browsable(
    htmltools::tagList(
      htmltools::tags$div(
        style = "max-width: 720px; margin: 8px 0 18px 0;",
        htmltools::tags$img(src = img_box, style = "width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px;")
      ),
      htmltools::tags$div(
        style = "max-width: 920px; margin: 8px 0 18px 0;",
        htmltools::tags$img(src = img_curve, style = "width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px;")
      )
    )
  )
}
```

## 5.2 Subject-Level

```{r accuracy-subj-table}
if (!is.null(params$accuracy_subj)) {
  params$accuracy_subj %>%
    dplyr::arrange(as.integer(participant_id)) %>%
    dplyr::rename(
      "Subject ID" = participant_id,
      "Mean Accuracy" = mean_accuracy,
      "N Valid Trials" = n_valid_trials
    ) %>%
    kable(digits = 3, caption = "Table 5.3: Subject-Level Accuracy") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    scroll_box(height = "500px")
} else {
  print("No subject-level accuracy provided.")
}
```

```{r accuracy-subj-plots, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(params$accuracy_subj)) {
  subj <- params$accuracy_subj %>%
    dplyr::mutate(below_chance = mean_accuracy < 0.5) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(mean_accuracy)

  n_subjects_accuracy <- nrow(subj)

  p_sorted <- ggplot(
    subj,
    aes(x = mean_accuracy, y = reorder(participant_id, mean_accuracy), color = below_chance)
  ) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.85) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", mean_accuracy), hjust = ifelse(mean_accuracy < 0.5, 1.3, -0.3)),
      vjust = 0.5,
      size = 3,
      check_overlap = TRUE
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "firebrick", "FALSE" = "black"), name = "Below Chance (0.5)") +
    labs(
      title = "Individual Participant Accuracy (Sorted by Accuracy)",
      subtitle = paste0("Each point represents a participant (N=", n_subjects_accuracy, ")."),
      x = "Mean Accuracy",
      y = "Participant ID"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "top",
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, color = "grey30")
    )

  p_by_id <- ggplot(
    subj,
    aes(x = mean_accuracy, y = factor(participant_id, levels = rev(sort(unique(participant_id)))), color = below_chance)
  ) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.85) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", mean_accuracy), hjust = ifelse(mean_accuracy < 0.5, 1.3, -0.3)),
      vjust = 0.5,
      size = 3,
      check_overlap = TRUE
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "firebrick", "FALSE" = "black"), name = "Below Chance (0.5)") +
    labs(
      title = "Individual Participant Accuracy (Sorted by ID)",
      subtitle = paste0("Each point represents a participant (N=", n_subjects_accuracy, ")."),
      x = "Mean Accuracy",
      y = "Participant ID"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "top",
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, color = "grey30")
    )

  tmp0 <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  tmp1 <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  ggsave(tmp0, plot = p_sorted, width = 8, height = max(4, 0.25 * n_subjects_accuracy), dpi = 160, limitsize = FALSE)
  ggsave(tmp1, plot = p_by_id, width = 8, height = max(4, 0.25 * n_subjects_accuracy), dpi = 160, limitsize = FALSE)

  img0 <- knitr::image_uri(tmp0)
  img1 <- knitr::image_uri(tmp1)

  carousel_id <- paste0("accuracy-carousel-", as.integer(stats::runif(1, 1, 1e9)))

  htmltools::browsable(
    htmltools::tagList(
      htmltools::tags$style(htmltools::HTML("
        .eda-carousel-wrap { max-width: 920px; margin: 8px 0 18px 0; }
        .eda-carousel { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; }
        .eda-carousel .viewport { flex: 1 1 auto; }
        .eda-carousel .slide { display: none; }
        .eda-carousel .slide.active { display: block; }
        .eda-carousel img { width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px; }
        .eda-carousel button.nav {
          width: 44px; height: 44px; border-radius: 22px;
          border: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.25);
          background: rgba(255,255,255,0.85);
          color: rgba(0,0,0,0.75);
          font-size: 26px; line-height: 40px;
          cursor: pointer;
          box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.12);
          user-select: none;
          flex: 0 0 auto;
        }
        .eda-carousel button.nav:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.95); }
        .eda-carousel .caption { text-align: center; margin-top: 6px; color: rgba(0,0,0,0.65); font-size: 14px; }
      ")),
      htmltools::tags$div(
        class = "eda-carousel-wrap",
        htmltools::tags$div(
          id = carousel_id,
          class = "eda-carousel",
          `data-idx` = "0",
          htmltools::tags$button(
            class = "nav prev",
            type = "button",
            onclick = sprintf("edaCarouselStep('%s', -1)", carousel_id),
            htmltools::HTML("&#10094;")
          ),
          htmltools::tags$div(
            class = "viewport",
            htmltools::tags$div(class = "slide active", htmltools::tags$img(src = img0, alt = "Accuracy plot (sorted by accuracy)")),
            htmltools::tags$div(class = "slide", htmltools::tags$img(src = img1, alt = "Accuracy plot (sorted by ID)"))
          ),
          htmltools::tags$button(
            class = "nav next",
            type = "button",
            onclick = sprintf("edaCarouselStep('%s', 1)", carousel_id),
            htmltools::HTML("&#10095;")
          )
        ),
        htmltools::tags$div(class = "caption", "Use arrows to switch: Sorted by Accuracy ↔ Sorted by ID")
      ),
      htmltools::tags$script(htmltools::HTML("
        function edaCarouselShow(id, idx) {
          const el = document.getElementById(id);
          if (!el) return;
          const slides = el.querySelectorAll('.slide');
          const n = slides.length;
          const i = ((idx % n) + n) % n;
          slides.forEach((s, k) => s.classList.toggle('active', k === i));
          el.setAttribute('data-idx', String(i));
        }
        function edaCarouselStep(id, delta) {
          const el = document.getElementById(id);
          if (!el) return;
          const cur = parseInt(el.getAttribute('data-idx') || '0', 10);
          edaCarouselShow(id, cur + delta);
        }
      "))
    )
  )
}
```

# 6. Choice Strategies

This section summarizes simple choice-strategy signatures:

- **Win-stay / lose-stay**: \(P(\text{stay} \mid \text{outcome on } t-1)\)
- **Post-outcome pauses (PRP)**: median reaction time on trial \(t\) conditioned on outcome on \(t-1\)

## 6.1 Win-stay / Lose-stay by Previous Outcome

```{r wsls-table}
if (!is.null(params$wsls_by_outcome_subj)) {
  df <- params$wsls_by_outcome_subj %>%
    dplyr::filter(outcome_type %in% c("Loss", "Non-Salient Win", "Salient Win")) %>%
    dplyr::select(participant_id, outcome_type, prob_stay) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      participant_id = as.character(participant_id),
      outcome_key = dplyr::case_when(
        outcome_type == "Loss" ~ "Loss",
        outcome_type == "Non-Salient Win" ~ "NW",
        outcome_type == "Salient Win" ~ "SW",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(outcome_key))

  wide <- df %>%
    dplyr::select(participant_id, outcome_key, prob_stay) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      id_cols = participant_id,
      names_from = outcome_key,
      values_from = prob_stay
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(as.integer(participant_id)) %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      "Subject ID" = participant_id,
      `\\(\\mathbf{P(Stay \\mid Loss)}\\)` = Loss,
      `\\(\\mathbf{P(Stay \\mid NW)}\\)` = NW,
      `\\(\\mathbf{P(Stay \\mid SW)}\\)` = SW
    )

  wide %>%
    kable(digits = 3, escape = FALSE, caption = "Table 6.1: Subject-Level Stay Probabilities by Previous Outcome") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    scroll_box(height = "500px")
} else {
  print("No WSLS data provided.")
}
```

```{r wsls-mean-table}
if (!is.null(params$wsls_by_outcome_subj)) {
  params$wsls_by_outcome_subj %>%
    dplyr::filter(outcome_type %in% c("Loss", "Non-Salient Win", "Salient Win")) %>%
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(prob_stay)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(outcome_type = factor(outcome_type, levels = c("Loss", "Non-Salient Win", "Salient Win"))) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(outcome_type) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      n_subjects = dplyr::n_distinct(participant_id),
      mean_prob_stay = mean(prob_stay, na.rm = TRUE),
      sd_prob_stay = stats::sd(prob_stay, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(outcome_type) %>%
    dplyr::rename(
      "Outcome Type (t-1)" = outcome_type,
      "N Subjects" = n_subjects,
      "Mean P(Stay)" = mean_prob_stay,
      "SD" = sd_prob_stay
    ) %>%
    kable(digits = 3, caption = "Table 6.1a: Mean Stay Probability by Previous Outcome (Across Subjects)") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
}
```

```{r wsls-test}
if (!is.null(params$wsls_test)) {
  params$wsls_test %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      `\\(\\mathbf{N}\\)` = n,
      `\\(\\mathbf{\\bar{x}_{SW}}\\)` = mean_a,
      `\\(\\mathbf{\\bar{x}_{NW}}\\)` = mean_b,
      `\\(\\mathbf{\\Delta}\\)` = mean_diff,
      `\\(\\mathbf{t}\\)` = t,
      `\\(\\mathbf{df}\\)` = df,
      `\\(\\mathbf{p}\\)` = p_value,
      `\\(\\mathbf{2.5\\%}\\)` = conf_low,
      `\\(\\mathbf{97.5\\%}\\)` = conf_high
    ) %>%
    kable(
      digits = 4,
      escape = FALSE,
      caption = "Table 6.2: Paired t-test of WSLS (Salient Win vs Non-Salient Win)"
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    row_spec(0, bold = FALSE)
} else {
  print("No WSLS test results provided.")
}
```

```{r wsls-test-loss-vs-wins}
if (!is.null(params$wsls_loss_vs_wins_test)) {
  params$wsls_loss_vs_wins_test %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      `\\(\\mathbf{N}\\)` = n,
      `\\(\\mathbf{\\bar{x}_{Loss}}\\)` = mean_a,
      `\\(\\mathbf{\\bar{x}_{Win}}\\)` = mean_b,
      `\\(\\mathbf{\\Delta}\\)` = mean_diff,
      `\\(\\mathbf{t}\\)` = t,
      `\\(\\mathbf{df}\\)` = df,
      `\\(\\mathbf{p}\\)` = p_value,
      `\\(\\mathbf{2.5\\%}\\)` = conf_low,
      `\\(\\mathbf{97.5\\%}\\)` = conf_high
    ) %>%
    kable(
      digits = 4,
      escape = FALSE,
      caption = "Table 6.2a: Paired t-test of WSLS (Loss vs All Wins)"
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    row_spec(0, bold = FALSE)
}
```
```{r wsls-plot, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(params$wsls_by_outcome_subj)) {
  df <- params$wsls_by_outcome_subj %>%
    dplyr::filter(outcome_type %in% c("Loss", "Non-Salient Win", "Salient Win")) %>%
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(prob_stay))

  if (nrow(df) > 0 && dplyr::n_distinct(df$participant_id) > 1) {
    df$outcome_type <- factor(df$outcome_type, levels = c("Loss", "Non-Salient Win", "Salient Win"))

    p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = outcome_type, y = prob_stay, fill = outcome_type)) +
      geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.5, color = "black", linewidth = 0.35) +
      geom_point(
        aes(group = participant_id),
        position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.1, dodge.width = 0.5),
        alpha = 0.35,
        size = 1.2
      ) +
      geom_line(
        aes(group = participant_id),
        color = "grey40",
        alpha = 0.25,
        position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.1, dodge.width = 0.5)
      ) +
      scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, 1)) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = c("Loss" = "grey70", "Non-Salient Win" = "white", "Salient Win" = "gold")) +
      labs(
        title = "Win-Stay Probability by Feedback Condition on Previous Trial",
        subtitle = "P(Stay | outcome on t-1). Points/lines are individual subjects.",
        x = "Outcome on Previous Trial (t-1)",
        y = "Probability of Staying with Previous Choice"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
      theme(legend.position = "none", plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

    tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
    ggsave(tmp, plot = p, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 170)
    img <- knitr::image_uri(tmp)

    htmltools::browsable(
      htmltools::tags$div(
        style = "max-width: 920px; margin: 8px 0 18px 0;",
        htmltools::tags$img(src = img, style = "width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px;")
      )
    )
  }
}
```

## 6.2 Post-outcome Pauses (PRP)

```{r prp-table}
if (!is.null(params$prp_by_outcome_subj)) {
  df <- params$prp_by_outcome_subj %>%
    dplyr::filter(outcome_type_prp %in% c("Post-Loss", "Post-Non-Salient Win", "Post-Salient Win")) %>%
    dplyr::select(participant_id, outcome_type_prp, median_prp) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      participant_id = as.character(participant_id),
      outcome_key = dplyr::case_when(
        outcome_type_prp == "Post-Loss" ~ "Loss",
        outcome_type_prp == "Post-Non-Salient Win" ~ "NW",
        outcome_type_prp == "Post-Salient Win" ~ "SW",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(outcome_key))

  wide <- df %>%
    dplyr::select(participant_id, outcome_key, median_prp) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      id_cols = participant_id,
      names_from = outcome_key,
      values_from = median_prp
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(as.integer(participant_id)) %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      "Subject ID" = participant_id,
      `\\(\\mathbf{Median\\ PRP_{Loss}}\\)` = Loss,
      `\\(\\mathbf{Median\\ PRP_{NW}}\\)` = NW,
      `\\(\\mathbf{Median\\ PRP_{SW}}\\)` = SW
    )

  wide %>%
    kable(
      digits = 3,
      escape = FALSE,
      caption = "Table 6.2: Subject-Level PRP (Median RT on Next Trial) by Previous Outcome"
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    scroll_box(height = "500px")
} else {
  print("No PRP data provided.")
}
```

```{r prp-test}
if (!is.null(params$prp_test)) {
  params$prp_test %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      `\\(\\mathbf{N}\\)` = n,
      `\\(\\mathbf{\\bar{x}_{SW}}\\)` = mean_a,
      `\\(\\mathbf{\\bar{x}_{NW}}\\)` = mean_b,
      `\\(\\mathbf{\\Delta}\\)` = mean_diff,
      `\\(\\mathbf{t}\\)` = t,
      `\\(\\mathbf{df}\\)` = df,
      `\\(\\mathbf{p}\\)` = p_value,
      `\\(\\mathbf{2.5\\%}\\)` = conf_low,
      `\\(\\mathbf{97.5\\%}\\)` = conf_high
    ) %>%
    kable(
      digits = 4,
      escape = FALSE,
      caption = "Table 6.3: Paired t-test of PRP (Post-Salient Win vs Post-Non-Salient Win)"
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    row_spec(0, bold = FALSE)
} else {
  print("No PRP test results provided.")
}
```

```{r prp-plot, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(params$prp_by_outcome_subj)) {
  df <- params$prp_by_outcome_subj %>%
    dplyr::filter(outcome_type_prp %in% c("Post-Non-Salient Win", "Post-Salient Win", "Post-Loss")) %>%
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(median_prp))

  if (nrow(df) > 0 && dplyr::n_distinct(df$participant_id) > 1) {
    df$outcome_type_prp <- factor(
      df$outcome_type_prp,
      levels = c("Post-Loss", "Post-Non-Salient Win", "Post-Salient Win")
    )

    p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = outcome_type_prp, y = median_prp, fill = outcome_type_prp)) +
      geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.55, color = "black", linewidth = 0.35) +
      geom_point(
        aes(group = participant_id),
        position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.1, dodge.width = 0.55),
        alpha = 0.35,
        size = 1.2
      ) +
      geom_line(
        aes(group = participant_id),
        color = "grey40",
        alpha = 0.25,
        position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.1, dodge.width = 0.55)
      ) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = c(
        "Post-Loss" = "grey70",
        "Post-Non-Salient Win" = "white",
        "Post-Salient Win" = "gold"
      )) +
      labs(
        title = "Post-Outcome Pauses (Median RT on Next Trial)",
        subtitle = "Each point is a subject's median RT following the specified outcome on the previous trial.",
        x = "Outcome on Previous Trial (t-1)",
        y = "Median Reaction Time on Current Trial (t) (seconds)"
      ) +
      eda_theme_plot2(base_size = 13) +
      theme(
        legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 10, hjust = 0.65)
      )

    tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
    ggsave(tmp, plot = p, width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 170)
    img <- knitr::image_uri(tmp)

    htmltools::browsable(
      htmltools::tags$div(
        style = "max-width: 980px; margin: 8px 0 18px 0;",
        htmltools::tags$img(src = img, style = "width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px;")
      )
    )
  }
}
```

# 7. Questionnaire Data (EDA)

This section provides descriptive exploration of questionnaire scores. We focus on total scores for impulsivity (BIS total) and sensation seeking (SSS total).

## 7.1 Subject-level scores

```{r questionnaire-subj-table}
if (!is.null(params$questionnaire_scores_subj)) {
  rename_map <- c(
    bis_total = "BIS Total",
    ss_total = "SSS Total",
    SST = "SST",
    SSE = "SSE",
    SSD = "SSD",
    SSB = "SSB"
  )

  params$questionnaire_scores_subj %>%
    dplyr::mutate(participant_id = as.character(participant_id)) %>%
    dplyr::rename("Subject ID" = participant_id) %>%
    dplyr::rename_with(.cols = dplyr::any_of(names(rename_map)), .fn = function(x) unname(rename_map[x])) %>%
    kable(digits = 2, caption = "Table 7.1: Questionnaire Scores by Subject (Totals + SSS Subscores)") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    scroll_box(height = "500px")
} else {
  print("No questionnaire data provided.")
}
```

## 7.2 Score distributions

```{r questionnaire-score-summaries}
if (!is.null(params$questionnaire_score_summaries)) {
  params$questionnaire_score_summaries %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      scale_name = dplyr::recode(
        scale_name,
        bis_total = "BIS Total",
        ss_total = "SSS Total"
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::rename(
      "Scale" = scale_name,
      "N" = n,
      "Mean" = mean,
      "Median" = median,
      "SD" = sd,
      "IQR" = iqr,
      "Min" = min,
      "Max" = max
    ) %>%
    kable(digits = 2, caption = "Table 7.2: Descriptive Statistics for Questionnaire Scores") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
} else {
  print("No questionnaire score summaries provided.")
}
```

```{r questionnaire-distribution-plots, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(params$questionnaire_scores_long)) {
  qlong <- params$questionnaire_scores_long %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      scale_name = dplyr::recode(
        scale_name,
        bis_total = "BIS Total",
        ss_total = "SSS Total"
      )
    )

  bis <- qlong %>% dplyr::filter(scale_name == "BIS Total") %>% dplyr::filter(is.finite(score))
  sss <- qlong %>% dplyr::filter(scale_name == "SSS Total") %>% dplyr::filter(is.finite(score))

  # Discrete binning (no gaps) + count y-axis, with scaled density overlay
  p_dist <- ggplot() +
    geom_histogram(
      data = bis,
      aes(x = score, y = after_stat(count)),
      binwidth = 1,
      center = 0.5,
      fill = "skyblue",
      color = "black",
      alpha = 0.75
    ) +
    geom_density(
      data = bis,
      aes(x = score, y = after_stat(count * 1)),
      color = "navy",
      linewidth = 0.9
    ) +
    geom_histogram(
      data = sss,
      aes(x = score, y = after_stat(count)),
      binwidth = 0.25,
      center = 0.125,
      fill = "skyblue",
      color = "black",
      alpha = 0.75
    ) +
    geom_density(
      data = sss,
      aes(x = score, y = after_stat(count * 0.25)),
      color = "navy",
      linewidth = 0.9
    ) +
    facet_wrap(~ scale_name, scales = "free_x") +
    labs(
      title = "Distribution of Key Questionnaire Scores",
      x = "Score",
      y = "N"
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      breaks = function(l) {
        m <- ceiling(max(l, na.rm = TRUE))
        if (!is.finite(m) || m <= 0) return(integer())
        seq(0, m, by = 2)
      }
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
    )

  p_box_bis <- bis %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = factor(1), y = score)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.colour = "red", fill = "skyblue") +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.25, size = 1.2) +
    labs(
      title = "Boxplot of BIS Total Scores",
      y = "BIS Total",
      x = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())

  p_box_sss <- sss %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = factor(1), y = score)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.colour = "red", fill = "skyblue") +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.25, size = 1.2) +
    labs(
      title = "Boxplot of SSS Total Scores",
      y = "SSS Total",
      x = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank())

  tmp1 <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  tmp2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  tmp3 <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  ggsave(tmp1, plot = p_dist, width = 10, height = 5, dpi = 170)
  ggsave(tmp2, plot = p_box_bis, width = 6.5, height = 5, dpi = 170)
  ggsave(tmp3, plot = p_box_sss, width = 6.5, height = 5, dpi = 170)

  img1 <- knitr::image_uri(tmp1)
  img2 <- knitr::image_uri(tmp2)
  img3 <- knitr::image_uri(tmp3)

  htmltools::browsable(
    htmltools::tagList(
      htmltools::tags$div(style = "max-width: 980px; margin: 8px 0 18px 0;",
                          htmltools::tags$img(src = img1, style = "width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px;")),
      htmltools::tags$div(
        style = "display: flex; gap: 14px; max-width: 980px; margin: 8px 0 18px 0; align-items: stretch; flex-wrap: wrap;",
        htmltools::tags$div(
          style = "flex: 1 1 360px;",
          htmltools::tags$img(src = img2, style = "width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px;")
        ),
        htmltools::tags$div(
          style = "flex: 1 1 360px;",
          htmltools::tags$img(src = img3, style = "width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px;")
        )
      )
    )
  )
}
```

## 7.3 BIS–SSS relationship

```{r questionnaire-bis-ss-plot, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(params$questionnaire_scores_subj) && !is.null(params$bis_ss_corr)) {
  df <- params$questionnaire_scores_subj %>%
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(bis_total) & is.finite(ss_total))

  corr <- params$bis_ss_corr
  r_txt <- if (!is.null(corr$r) && is.finite(corr$r[1])) sprintf("r = %.2f", corr$r[1]) else "r = NA"
  p_txt <- if (!is.null(corr$p_value) && is.finite(corr$p_value[1])) scales::pvalue(corr$p_value[1], accuracy = .001, add_p = TRUE) else "p = NA"

  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = bis_total, y = ss_total)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.65, color = "purple") +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", se = FALSE, linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(
      title = "Relationship between BIS Total and SSS Total Scores",
      subtitle = paste0(r_txt, ", ", p_txt),
      x = "BIS Total Score",
      y = "SSS Total Score"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey30"))

  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png")
  ggsave(tmp, plot = p, width = 7, height = 6, dpi = 170)
  img <- knitr::image_uri(tmp)

  htmltools::browsable(
    htmltools::tags$div(
      style = "max-width: 820px; margin: 8px 0 18px 0;",
      htmltools::tags$img(src = img, style = "width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px;")
    )
  )
}
```

```{r questionnaire-bis-ss-tables}
if (!is.null(params$bis_ss_corr)) {
  params$bis_ss_corr %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      `\\(\\mathbf{N}\\)` = n,
      `\\(\\mathbf{r}\\)` = r,
      `\\(\\mathbf{t}\\)` = t,
      `\\(\\mathbf{df}\\)` = df,
      `\\(\\mathbf{p}\\)` = p_value,
      `\\(\\mathbf{CI}_{95\\%}^{low}\\)` = conf_low,
      `\\(\\mathbf{CI}_{95\\%}^{high}\\)` = conf_high
    ) %>%
    kable(digits = 4, escape = FALSE, caption = "Table 7.3: Pearson Correlation (BIS Total vs SSS Total)") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    row_spec(0, bold = FALSE)
}

if (!is.null(params$bis_ss_lm)) {
  params$bis_ss_lm %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      `\\(\\mathbf{N}\\)` = n,
      `\\(\\mathbf{Intercept}\\)` = intercept,
      `\\(\\mathbf{Slope}\\)` = slope,
      `\\(\\mathbf{R^2}\\)` = r_squared,
      `\\(\\mathbf{p_{slope}}\\)` = p_slope
    ) %>%
    kable(digits = 4, escape = FALSE, caption = "Table 7.4: Linear Model (SSS Total ~ BIS Total)") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    row_spec(0, bold = FALSE)
}
```

# 8. Appendix (Participant-level Diagnostics)

This appendix embeds participant-level inspection outputs directly into this HTML file for deep-dive exploration. Use the buttons below to expand/collapse all participants.

```{r appendix-controls, results='asis'}
htmltools::browsable(
  htmltools::tagList(
    htmltools::tags$style(htmltools::HTML("
      .eda-appendix-controls { display: flex; gap: 8px; margin: 10px 0 12px 0; }
      .eda-appendix-controls button {
        border: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.25);
        background: rgba(255,255,255,0.9);
        padding: 6px 10px;
        border-radius: 6px;
        cursor: pointer;
      }
      .eda-appendix-controls button:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,1); }
      details.eda-participant { border: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.10); border-radius: 8px; padding: 6px 10px; margin: 8px 0; background: rgba(250,250,250,0.7); }
      details.eda-participant summary { cursor: pointer; font-weight: 600; }
      .eda-participant-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr; gap: 10px; margin-top: 10px; }
      .eda-participant-card { background: white; border: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.08); border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px; }
      .eda-participant-card .title { font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 6px; color: rgba(0,0,0,0.75); }
      .eda-participant-card img { width: 100%; height: auto; border-radius: 6px; }
      .eda-wide-scroll { overflow-x: auto; width: 100%; padding-bottom: 8px; }
    ")),
    htmltools::tags$div(
      class = "eda-appendix-controls",
      htmltools::tags$button(type = "button", onclick = "edaSetAllDetails(true)", "Expand all"),
      htmltools::tags$button(type = "button", onclick = "edaSetAllDetails(false)", "Collapse all")
    ),
    htmltools::tags$script(htmltools::HTML("
      function edaSetAllDetails(open) {
        const els = document.querySelectorAll('details.eda-participant');
        els.forEach(d => { d.open = open; });
      }
    "))
  )
)
```

## 8.1 Per-participant inspection plots (Reward probabilities + trial-wise outcomes)

```{r appendix-participant-plots, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(params$cleaning_stats_subj)) {
  pids <- as.character(params$cleaning_stats_subj$participant_id)
  pids <- pids[order(as.integer(pids))]

  make_img <- function(path) {
    if (!file.exists(path)) return(NULL)
    knitr::image_uri(path)
  }

  blocks <- lapply(pids, function(pid) {
    reward_prob_path <- file.path(
      "analysis", "outputs", "figs", "inspection", "participant_wise",
      "combined_reward_saliency", paste0("combined_reward_probs_subject_", pid, ".png")
    )

    img1 <- make_img(reward_prob_path)

    htmltools::tags$details(
      class = "eda-participant",
      htmltools::tags$summary(paste0("Participant ", pid)),
      htmltools::tags$div(
        class = "eda-participant-grid",
        htmltools::tags$div(
          class = "eda-participant-card",
          htmltools::tags$div(class = "title", "Reward probabilities + trial-wise outcomes"),
          if (!is.null(img1)) htmltools::tags$img(src = img1) else htmltools::tags$div("Plot not found.")
        )
      )
    )
  })

  htmltools::browsable(htmltools::tagList(blocks))
}
```

## 8.2 Participant behavioral summary (full table)

This table is intentionally wide (participants as columns). Use horizontal scrolling to inspect all participants.

```{r appendix-summary-table, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(params$stim_pref_subj) &&
    !is.null(params$accuracy_subj) &&
    !is.null(params$reward_rate_subj) &&
    !is.null(params$win_stay_overall_subj)) {

  stim <- params$stim_pref_subj %>%
    dplyr::select(participant_id, prop_choice_0, prop_choice_1)
  acc <- params$accuracy_subj %>%
    dplyr::select(participant_id, mean_accuracy)
  rr <- params$reward_rate_subj %>%
    dplyr::select(participant_id, reward_rate)
  ws <- params$win_stay_overall_subj %>%
    dplyr::select(participant_id, win_stay_prob)

  metrics_long <- stim %>%
    dplyr::left_join(rr, by = "participant_id") %>%
    dplyr::left_join(acc, by = "participant_id") %>%
    dplyr::left_join(ws, by = "participant_id") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(participant_id = as.character(participant_id)) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = c(prop_choice_0, prop_choice_1, reward_rate, mean_accuracy, win_stay_prob),
      names_to = "Metric",
      values_to = "Value"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Metric = dplyr::recode(
        Metric,
        prop_choice_0 = "p(S0)",
        prop_choice_1 = "p(S1)",
        reward_rate = "Reward Rate",
        mean_accuracy = "Accuracy",
        win_stay_prob = "p(Win-Stay)"
      )
    )

  wide <- metrics_long %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = participant_id, values_from = Value) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(factor(Metric, levels = c("p(S0)", "p(S1)", "Reward Rate", "Accuracy", "p(Win-Stay)")))

  participant_cols <- setdiff(names(wide), "Metric")

  # ---- Row-specific heatmap styling (minimal dependencies: base + kableExtra) ----
  .col_lerp <- function(col1, col2, t) {
    ramp <- grDevices::colorRamp(c(col1, col2))
    rgb <- ramp(max(0, min(1, t))) / 255
    grDevices::rgb(rgb[1], rgb[2], rgb[3])
  }

  .col_diverging <- function(x, vmin, mid, vmax, col_low, col_mid = "white", col_high) {
    if (!is.finite(x)) return(NA_character_)
    if (!is.finite(vmin) || !is.finite(vmax)) return(NA_character_)
    if (vmin == vmax) return(col_mid)

    if (x <= mid) {
      denom <- (mid - vmin)
      t <- if (denom <= 0) 0.5 else (x - vmin) / denom
      return(.col_lerp(col_low, col_mid, t))
    } else {
      denom <- (vmax - mid)
      t <- if (denom <= 0) 0.5 else (x - mid) / denom
      return(.col_lerp(col_mid, col_high, t))
    }
  }

  .col_minmax <- function(x, vmin, vmax, col_low = "white", col_high = "#2ca25f") {
    if (!is.finite(x)) return(NA_character_)
    if (!is.finite(vmin) || !is.finite(vmax)) return(NA_character_)
    if (vmin == vmax) return(.col_lerp(col_low, col_high, 0.5))
    t <- (x - vmin) / (vmax - vmin)
    .col_lerp(col_low, col_high, t)
  }

  wide_num <- wide
  for (cn in participant_cols) wide_num[[cn]] <- as.numeric(wide_num[[cn]])

  wide_styled <- wide
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(wide))) {
    metric <- wide$Metric[i]
    vals <- as.numeric(wide_num[i, participant_cols, drop = TRUE])
    vmin <- suppressWarnings(min(vals, na.rm = TRUE))
    vmax <- suppressWarnings(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE))

    for (cn in participant_cols) {
      x <- wide_num[[cn]][i]
      if (!is.finite(x)) {
        wide_styled[[cn]][i] <- ""
        next
      }

      bg <- if (metric == "p(S0)") {
        .col_diverging(x, vmin = vmin, mid = 0.5, vmax = vmax, col_low = "firebrick", col_high = "dodgerblue")
      } else if (metric == "p(S1)") {
        .col_diverging(x, vmin = vmin, mid = 0.5, vmax = vmax, col_low = "dodgerblue", col_high = "firebrick")
      } else {
        .col_minmax(x, vmin = vmin, vmax = vmax, col_low = "white", col_high = "#2ca25f")
      }

      wide_styled[[cn]][i] <- kableExtra::cell_spec(sprintf("%.3f", x), background = bg, color = "black")
    }
  }

  tbl <- wide_styled %>%
    knitr::kable(
      format = "html",
      escape = FALSE,
      caption = "Table 8.1: Participant Behavioral Summary (Wide)"
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(
      bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
      full_width = TRUE,
      font_size = 12
    )

  htmltools::browsable(
    htmltools::tags$div(
      class = "eda-wide-scroll",
      htmltools::HTML(as.character(tbl))
    )
  )
}
```
